action.reasoning: Observation: the h192/l4 12-epoch pure PyTorch PairMPNN improved test MAE from 0.3545 to 0.1364 with val MAE 0.1347, so the stable 3D distance model is now competitive but still far from geometric-DL potential. Analysis: the current message function does not condition messages directly on the receiver atom embedding, limiting pair-type/context expressiveness. A modest architectural upgrade using edge MLP inputs [h_i, h_j, radial_basis(distance)] should improve expressivity while staying dependency-free and within time. Decision: add a backward-compatible PairMPNNv2 architecture, update train/predict to select arch from checkpoint config, train v2 h192/l4 for 10 epochs, smoke-test predict.py, and submit evaluation.
action.command: python train.py --arch pairmpnn_v2 --hidden_dim 192 --num_layers 4 --num_rbf 48 --epochs 10 --batch_size 256 --lr 7e-4 --weight_decay 1e-6 --out checkpoints/pairmpnn_v2_h192_l4_e10.pt && python predict.py --data_path smoke --checkpoint checkpoints/pairmpnn_v2_h192_l4_e10.pt --output smoke/out_v2.json
